examples.title: Iterative Binary Search in C
examples.description: Implementation of Binary Search using iterative approach with O(1) space complexity
examples.explanation: Uses three variables (low, high, mid) to track search space. Mid is recalculated each iteration. If target equals mid, return index; if target > mid, search right half by setting low = mid + 1; if target < mid, search left half by setting high = mid - 1. Returns -1 when low > high (search space exhausted).

examples.title: Recursive Binary Search in C
examples.description: Implementation of Binary Search using recursion with O(log n) space complexity
examples.explanation: Base case handles element not found (low > high). Recursively calls itself with reduced search space. Each recursive call adds a stack frame. The function either finds the element at mid or reduces search space by half. Space complexity is O(log n) due to recursion depth.

examples.title: Binary Search with First Occurrence for Duplicates
examples.description: Modified Binary Search to find first occurrence of duplicate elements
examples.code:
def binary_search_first(arr, target):
    """Find first occurrence of target in sorted array with duplicates"""
    result = -1  # Store the leftmost position found
    low, high = 0, len(arr) - 1
    
    while low <= high:
        mid = low + (high - low) // 2
        
        if arr[mid] == target:
            result = mid  # Record position
            high = mid - 1  # Continue searching left for earlier occurrence
        elif arr[mid] < target:
            low = mid + 1
        else:
            high = mid - 1
    
    return result

# Example usage
arr = [2, 3, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6, 7]
target = 3
index = binary_search_first(arr, target)
print(f"First occurrence of {target} is at index: {index}")  # Output: 1
examples.explanation: When arr[mid] == target, instead of returning immediately, we record the position and continue searching the left half (high = mid - 1) to find if there's an earlier occurrence. This ensures we find the leftmost element when duplicates exist.

examples.title: Binary Search for Multiple Test Cases
examples.description: Complete C program demonstrating Binary Search with various test cases
examples.explanation: This complete program demonstrates Binary Search with 5 test cases: element in middle (23), first element (2), last element (91), element not present (100), and element at index 4 (16). Output validates the algorithm works correctly for all scenarios.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) per search, O(5*log n) for all test cases
